# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Service configuration that exposes our application to the internet using a LoadBalancer. The app serves HTTP traffic on port 80 and HTTPS on port 443. Make it accessible from any source for maximum flexibility. This is for our public-facing API.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{your_service}}
spec:
  type: {{type}} # LoadBalancer
  selector:
    app: {{your_app}} # your-app-name
    component: {{your_component}} # your-componet-name
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: http 
  - port: 443 
    targetPort: https

Note that LoadBalancer is supported only for GKE. If you're using other Kubernetes environments, use the type field in the Service specification to set the type of service. If it is not specified at all (the default), this will fail as this is a required field.
